title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's slight edge in trader consensus at 17.8% stems from their flawless UEFA qualifiers, Euro 2024 title defense momentum, and favorable Group H draw against Uruguay, Cape Verde, and Saudi Arabia, capped by a 3-0 friendly win over Serbia last week. France (16.1%) trails closely on Mbappé's form and Nations League success, while England's 11.3% reflects an unbeaten qualifying campaign despite a 1-1 draw versus Uruguay. Argentina and Brazil (8.8% and 8.6%) hold firm via CONMEBOL dominance and Messi/Neymar pedigree, keeping the top cluster tight amid the expanded 48-team field's parity—elite squads across UEFA and CONMEBOL face manageable group paths but grueling knockouts, with no major injuries disrupting rosters two months pre-tournament.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's Tisza Party secured a projected landslide victory in Hungary's April 12 parliamentary election, winning an estimated 135 of 199 seats in the National Assembly based on partial vote tallies, prompting incumbent Prime Minister Viktor Orbán to concede defeat after 16 years in power and personally congratulate his former admirer-turned-rival. This overwhelming mandate, reflecting widespread voter backlash against Fidesz amid economic pressures and corruption allegations, has driven trader consensus to price Magyar as near-certain next prime minister, with formal government formation expected following the new parliament's convening. While procedural hurdles like final certifications remain, only extraordinary challenges such as successful recounts or legal disputes could realistically shift the outcome given the decisive margin.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination due to commanding showings in recent polls, including a March Politico survey giving him a 14-point edge over Kamala Harris in their home-state primary and Emerson's national poll placing him atop the early field amid his high-profile clashes with the Trump administration. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows with progressive appeal amplified by her February Munich Security Conference appearance and youth, while Jon Ossoff and Harris tie amid Senate exposure and incumbency questions. In this fragmented primary, governors like Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg differentiate via executive records and swing-state appeal; consolidation may follow 2026 midterm outcomes, fundraising edges, and endorsements ahead of Iowa and New Hampshire.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: In Peru's April 12 first-round presidential election—marked by a record 35 candidates and voter fatigue from nine presidents in a decade—trader consensus positions Keiko Fujimori as the frontrunner to claim the presidency, driven by exit polls from Ipsos showing her at around 17% and her enduring name recognition from 2011 and 2016 runs, bolstered by Fuerza Popular's congressional strength. Rafael López Aliaga's early official counts leading at 20-23% reflect his appeal as a right-leaning businessman emphasizing security amid rising crime, while Jorge Nieto's third-place surge to 16% stems from recent polling gains in a fragmented field ensuring a June runoff. No candidate neared 50%, heightening uncertainty as vote tallies continue.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 7 - April 14, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 7 12:00 PM ET to April 14, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters around 300-359 tweets for Elon Musk's X posts from April 7-14, reflecting his blistering pace of 261 posts through 86% of the seven-day window as of early April 13, averaging over 40 daily amid a recent surge topping 60 per day in the prior 48 hours. This elevated volume—up from prior week's 30-40 average—stems from heightened platform engagement during viral cultural moments, positioning 320-339 as the slim frontrunner at 29.9% implied probability. The tight race with 300-319 (24.4%) and 340-359 (21.3%) hinges on volatile patterns: tweet storms or lulls in the final stretch to April 14 noon ET could swing outcomes, underscoring prediction markets' wisdom in pricing Elon's unpredictable social media rhythm.

title: Hawks vs. Heat
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:00PM ET:
If the Hawks win, the market will resolve to "Hawks".
If the Heat win, the market will resolve to "Heat".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Spread: Heat (-8.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Heat" if the Heat win the game by 9 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Hawks". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Hawks".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Hawks vs. Heat: O/U 239.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Hawks and Heat combine to score 240 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 240, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Tyler Herro: Points O/U 5.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Tyler Herro scores more than 5.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Tyler Herro scores 5.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlanta Hawks face the Miami Heat in the regular-season finale on April 12, with Hawks (46-35) having clinched a top-six Eastern Conference playoff seed after a recent win over Cleveland, prompting rest for key players like Jalen Johnson, CJ McCollum, and others including Jonathan Kuminga (knee), Onyeka Okongwu (finger), and Dyson Daniels (toe), alongside multiple outs for injuries. Miami (43-39), fighting for optimal play-in tournament positioning at home, boasts a healthier roster, with only Nikola Jovic (ankle) and Dru Smith (foot) confirmed out, Norman Powell (groin) questionable, and Simone Fontecchio probable. Heat hold historical head-to-head edge, but Hawks' depleted lineup shifts momentum toward Miami amid end-of-season load management and seeding stakes.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Derrick Jones Jr. scores more than 2.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Derrick Jones Jr. scores 2.5 points or fewer during the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kawhi Leonard's absence due to a left ankle sprain headlines the Clippers' injury report for their home regular-season finale against the Warriors, significantly weakening LA's lineup amid Bradley Beal's season-ending hip injury and other outs like Isaiah Jackson (ankle). Golden State enters with Draymond Green questionable (low back injury management), Quinten Post out (foot), and LJ Cryer sidelined (ankle), but Stephen Curry probable and near full strength otherwise. With Clippers (41-40) holding the West's No. 9 seed and Warriors (37-44) at No. 10, this Pacific Division clash serves as a play-in tune-up, potentially previewing a 9-10 showdown; recent 24-hour reports underscore rest advantages and seeding stakes driving trader consensus on a competitive matchup.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $85 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and the final trading day during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices hovered near $114 per barrel on April 13, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on sustained geopolitical risks outweighing supply pressures after OPEC+ agreed on April 5 to a modest 206,000 bpd output hike starting May—largely symbolic given member constraints. The EIA's April 8 report revealed a 3.1 million barrel crude inventory build to 464.7 million barrels for the week ending April 3, curbing bullish momentum, while prices rebounded from an April 8 plunge triggered by a U.S.-Iran de-escalation deal. Key dynamics include Strait of Hormuz disruptions and robust U.S. exports amid European sanctions; traders monitor weekly EIA releases through late April and potential Middle East flare-ups for resolution-defining swings.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 98.2% implied probability of no change in the Fed funds rate target range of 3.50%-3.75% at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on a resilient U.S. economy amid sticky inflation pressures. The March 17-18 FOMC held rates steady, with updated dot plot projections showing modestly higher 2026 inflation expectations, stable Q4 unemployment at 4.4%, and solid GDP growth; recent March CPI revealed a 12.5% year-over-year energy surge, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.3% amid labor market stabilization per Fed speeches. This skin-in-the-game sentiment leaves scant room for a 25 basis point cut or hike barring unforeseen shocks like accelerating disinflation or geopolitical oil spikes ahead of the decision.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-17 record and No. 1 Western Conference seed have solidified their 41.5% implied probability as 2026 NBA champions, fueled by a 19-1 surge before a recent loss to Denver, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play averaging 31+ points, and full health entering playoffs after Jalen Williams' return. San Antonio Spurs trail at 15.8% with a 62-19 mark and 9-1 recent run, powered by Victor Wembanyama's dominance and franchise-best start as NBA Cup runners-up. Boston Celtics (11.4%) leverage defending champion experience despite a 55-26 record, while Denver Nuggets (10.5%) ride an 11-game win streak and Nikola Jokic's league-leading rebounds-assists double-double amid widespread end-of-season rest for contenders. Detroit Pistons' Eastern lead at 59+ wins positions them at 5.9%, highlighting a tightly contested playoff path.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% to lead the Republican Presidential Nominee 2028 race, closely trailed by Vice President J.D. Vance at 40.3% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.9%, capturing uncertainty over Trump administration insiders' paths to victory amid $544 million in volume. Kennedy's HHS role and Make America Healthy Again movement fueled a recent 34-point odds surge from heavy trading, while Vance topped the CPAC straw poll at 53% and released a book signaling ambitions; Rubio gained from donor preferences in Trump polling. Midterms, executive performance, Trump endorsements, and early primary dynamics could tip this contested field.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors Vice President JD Vance at 19% implied probability to win the 2028 presidential election, ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17%, reflecting the GOP's incumbency advantage following President Trump's second term amid constitutional term limits barring his re-run. Vance's lead narrowed sharply to an all-time low in late March 2026, coinciding with reports of major donors preferring Senator Marco Rubio—now at 10%—as a potential alternative after Trump polled preferences, while Rubio's odds rose earlier from diplomatic actions like the Venezuela strike. The fragmented field across both parties keeps the race competitive, with 2026 midterms in swing states poised to signal nominee viability through turnout, battleground performance, and early primary indicators.

title: Military action against Iran ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends by

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan took effect on April 8 between the US, Israel, and Iran following intense airstrikes under Operation Epic Fury that began February 28, severely degrading Iran's air force, navy, ballistic missile production, air defenses, and nuclear-related sites. Marathon US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad concluded without agreement on April 12, prompting President Trump to announce and implement a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on April 13 amid Iran's continued disruptions to maritime traffic. No major airstrikes have been reported since early April, but the truce faces strain from proxy attacks, Hezbollah operations in Lebanon, and Gulf infrastructure strikes, with traders monitoring a 48-hour confirmation window for potential market resolutions and risks of escalation over navigation rights.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: The collapse of Pakistan-brokered US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad on April 12 has intensified uncertainty around the fragile two-week ceasefire agreed April 7, as Israeli airstrikes continue against Hezbollah in Lebanon—actions Tehran claims violate the pause. Following US and Israeli attacks on Iranian nuclear sites, IRGC assets, and economic targets since late February, trader sentiment reflects de-escalation risks amid President Trump's threats of a Strait of Hormuz blockade and warnings to China over arms shipments. The ceasefire may expire around April 21 without a deal, with potential for renewed airstrikes, missile barrages, or diplomatic breakthroughs shaping whether the conflict formally ends.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell holds the edge in trader consensus at 44.5% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, fueled by his consistent podium finishes—including a win in the Australian Grand Prix—and experience as the team's veteran against rookie sensation Kimi Antonelli's hot start at 30.1%. Antonelli leads the standings 72-63 after back-to-back victories in China and Japan, where he claimed pole and fastest laps, showcasing Mercedes' early-season dominance with a 45-point constructors' lead over Ferrari. Recent intra-team pressure mounts as team principal Toto Wolff greenlights a title duel without orders, while Russell trails by nine points amid calls for aggressive racing; Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (6.3%) lags in the slower car, and Red Bull's Max Verstappen (2.5%) struggles with reliability.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland commands a commanding 38.5% implied probability as the Eurovision 2026 frontrunner on Polymarket, propelled by the electrifying live reception of Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin" at recent pre-contest showcases like Eurovision In Concert, where the classical violin-pop fusion dazzled crowds and reinforced their UMK national final triumph. France's Monroe with "Regarde!" (12.3%) and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund's "Før vi går hjem" (11.1%) trail closely, buoyed by jury-friendly ballads and strong DMGP/selection momentum, while Australia's Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") holds at 6.0% amid televote appeal. With first rehearsals imminent ahead of Vienna semis on May 12-14, trader consensus reflects promo tour buzz but eyes jury-televote splits and running order impacts for potential shifts.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 11 - April 13, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 11 12:00 PM ET to April 13, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 115-139 tweets (43%) and 140-164 (50.2%) for Elon Musk's X activity from April 11-13, reflecting his explosive recent pace of about 60 posts per day over the first two days, totaling 120 as of early April 13. This surge stems from viral threads amplifying South African "racist laws," Hungary election upsets involving globalist figures like Soros, and jabs at legacy media credibility, fueling massive engagement and rapid-fire replies. Key swing factor: Musk's posting tempo on the final day, potentially accelerated by today's Starlink launch, with historical patterns showing event-driven spikes amid his pop culture dominance on politics and tech. Resolution hinges on midday ET cutoff, where sustained momentum could tip higher.

title: Pelicans vs. Timberwolves
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Pelicans win, the market will resolve to "Pelicans".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the NBA regular season finale at Target Center, trader consensus favors the Timberwolves as slim home favorites despite resting key contributors like Anthony Edwards (right knee maintenance), Rudy Gobert (rest), Julius Randle (thumb), Naz Reid (shoulder maintenance), and others, with Minnesota's No. 6 Western Conference seed locked in ahead of playoffs. The Pelicans, eliminated at 26-55 and playing for draft position, counter with their own decimated roster including Zion Williamson, Dejounte Murray, Trey Murphy III, Herb Jones, and six more out, crippling their already poor recent form. Minnesota's five wins in six games, including sweeps of New Orleans this season, and home-court advantage shape the closely contested implied probabilities, though both benches introduce upset potential.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 13?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters tightly around 300-359 posts for Elon Musk's X activity from April 10-17, with 320-339 leading at 15.4%, driven by his recent surge to 60 posts per day over April 11-13 amid viral political commentary on South African farmer murders, Hungarian election upsets, and media critiques. This pace—projecting 367 total if sustained—reflects heightened engagement with global controversies, up from prior weeks' 40-50 daily average, as tracked by X analytics. The close contest among top bins underscores Elon's variable output, swayed by breaking news or company updates like Tesla or SpaceX; watch April 14-17 for potential spikes from U.S. policy reactions or personal statements that could push toward 340+ or pull below 300.

title: Kings vs. Trail Blazers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Kings win, the market will resolve to "Kings".
If the Trail Blazers win, the market will resolve to "Trail Blazers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Spread: Trail Blazers (-17.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Trail Blazers" if the Trail Blazers win the game by 18 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Kings". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Kings".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Deni Avdija: Points O/U 8.5
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Portland Trail Blazers host Sacramento Kings in the 2025-26 NBA regular season finale at Moda Center, with Portland (41-40) needing a victory to clinch the No. 8 Western Conference seed for the play-in tournament against Phoenix Suns, leveraging their tiebreaker over the Lakers. Kings (21-59), locked into lottery contention after a dismal campaign, submit an extensive injury report ruling out DeMar DeRozan (hamstring), Zach LaVine (finger), Domantas Sabonis, Malik Monk, Russell Westbrook (right toe), Keegan Murray (ankle), and others—effectively resting stars on the second night of a back-to-back road trip. Blazers counter absences of Jerami Grant (calf), Jrue Holiday (calf), and Scoot Henderson, though Shaedon Sharpe's recent return from injury adds scoring punch; Portland swept the 3-0 season series head-to-head. Recent 24-hour updates confirm Kings' depleted depth, tilting trader sentiment toward Blazers' home motivation despite mutual roster hits.

title: 2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
description:
The 2026 Seoul mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Seoul.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chong Won-oh's commanding 87.5% implied probability in the 2026 Seoul mayoral election prediction market stems from his April 9 confirmation as the Democratic Party of Korea's candidate, securing over 50% in the party's primary combining member votes and general polls. Recent head-to-head surveys show him leading incumbent Mayor Oh Se-hoon by wide margins, such as 55.8% to 32.4%, amid Democratic advantages in Seoul's voter base. Oh Se-hoon holds 8% as the People Power Party frontrunner after registering for nomination, but party infighting and lagging polls temper expectations. With the June 3 local election approaching, traders' skin-in-the-game consensus favors Chong unless PPP unifies behind a stronger challenger or polls shift dramatically.

title: LoL: Sentinels vs Cloud9 (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against Cloud9.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Sentinels and Cloud9. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: LoL: Sentinels vs Cloud9 (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against Cloud9.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

markets.question: Game Handicap: SEN (-1.5) vs Cloud9 (+1.5)
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Cloud9 enters as the higher-seeded contender in LCS Spring 2026 standings after a strong Lock-In run, bolstered by veterans like Blaber, Vulcan, and Zven, though recent series reveal sloppy macro with squandered leads and mental resets needed post-tight wins. Sentinels' revamped 2026 roster—Impact top, Hambak jungle, Darkwings mid, Rahel ADC, Huhi support—shows early-game cohesion in Week 2 play but remains unproven in BO3 regular season grind, coming off a 1-2 loss to C9 on April 12 amid roster integration. Head-to-head favors Cloud9 from February Lock-In semis; no reported subs or injuries shift dynamics, with patch meta emphasizing objective control key for playoff positioning.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US strikes targeted military sites on Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal on April 7, 2026, ahead of President Trump's expired deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but spared critical oil infrastructure handling 90% of Iran's crude exports, which Iranian reports confirm remains operational and fully controlled by local forces. Earlier March airstrikes similarly avoided energy assets amid escalating US-Iran hostilities, with Trump publicly musing about potential seizure to pressure regime finances, though experts highlight risks of prolonged ground operations and retaliation. No verified loss of Iranian control has occurred, sustaining trader caution; provisional ceasefire talks and Hormuz naval dynamics could influence near-term escalation, with resolution hinging on confirmed territorial shifts by market deadlines.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 97%, reflecting the Iranian regime's demonstrated resilience amid severe economic strain and ongoing protests since late 2025. Security forces loyal to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, installed March 9 after Ali Khamenei's February assassination in US-Israeli strikes, have suppressed unrest through mass arrests, internet blackouts, and crackdowns without reported defections or retreats—mirroring the January 2026 massacre that quelled the initial wave. The April 12 collapse of US-Iran talks in Islamabad has prompted pro-regime rallies and defiance, offsetting $300 billion-plus war damages and 40% inflation surge. With just weeks to April 30, historical patterns favor stability barring elite mutiny, uncontrollable uprisings, or full-scale invasion.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on April 12 following the collapse of marathon peace talks in Islamabad, signaling no immediate end to military pressure on Iran despite a two-week ceasefire agreed on April 7 that suspended airstrikes. The administration claims decisive gains, including destruction of Iran's navy, air force, and missile capabilities, positioning Tehran as weakened without nuclear prospects, yet Trump warned of resuming limited strikes if negotiations fail. Traders weigh ongoing diplomatic efforts against escalation risks, with oil prices surpassing $100 amid uncertainty over resolution timelines or full de-escalation announcements.

title: Utah vs. Flames
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the Flames win, the market will resolve to "Flames".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah Mammoth head to Calgary for a crucial late-season matchup against the Flames, vying to solidify their wild-card standing in the final road game before the Stanley Cup Playoffs, while the Flames—mired in the Pacific Division basement around 30th overall—aim to play spoiler amid a three-game losing streak. Utah boasts a stronger record at 32-31-10 through 79 games, with recent momentum including clinching a playoff spot, though starting goaltender Karel Vejmelka sat out with an undisclosed injury, forcing Vitek Vanecek into net. Calgary leans on Dustin Wolf in goal at home (21-12-5 record), but faces absences like Yan Kuznetsov (upper body) and Matvei Gridin (illness), plus longer-term injuries to Huberdeau and others; head-to-head splits are even this season, heightening upset potential in this low-stakes tilt for the Flames.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Direct US-Iran talks in Islamabad on April 11-12 marked the highest-level diplomatic meeting between the two nations since 1979, with Vice President JD Vance engaging Iran's Parliament Speaker amid a fragile two-week ceasefire following weeks of hostilities. Negotiations stalled after 21 hours over key sticking points including Strait of Hormuz access, sanctions relief, nuclear program commitments, and ceasefire extension to Lebanon, prompting US announcements of an impending naval blockade on Iranian ports. No further rounds are immediately scheduled, though regional mediators like Pakistan continue backchannel efforts; trader consensus reflects uncertainty in de-escalation amid mutual recriminations and escalation signals.

title: Canucks vs. Ducks
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Canucks win, the market will resolve to "Canucks".
If the Ducks win, the market will resolve to "Ducks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anaheim Ducks host the struggling Vancouver Canucks in a Pacific Division clash with playoff implications for the home side, as traders weigh the Ducks' solid 42-32-5 record and third-place standing against Vancouver's dismal 23-48-8 mark and eighth-in-West position. Key factors include Ducks forward Cutter Gauthier's return from upper-body injury, adding firepower with 38 goals this season, while Anaheim's goaltender Lukas Dostal faces Kevin Lankinen amid Vancouver's absences of Thatcher Demko, Evander Kane, Filip Chytil, and Derek Forbort. Ducks boast 5-3-2 form over their last 10 with a 6-1 thrashing of San Jose, contrasting Canucks' 2-8-0 skid; home-ice edge (23-10-3) and recent 5-3 win over Vancouver further tilt sentiment toward Anaheim's trader consensus.
